prompt: In Vendor Risk Management, what is the primary purpose of using questionnaires?
choices: Replace all contract reviews
Collect structured control evidence from vendors against defined risk domains
Close vendor risks without analysis
Generate invoices for assessments
explanation: Questionnaires standardize evidence collection and help compare vendor controls consistently.

prompt: Which questionnaire design choice improves response quality in TPRM?
choices: Use only open-text questions for all sections
Map questions to clear control statements and expected evidence types
Avoid conditional branching
Send the same questionnaire to every vendor category
explanation: Control-mapped questions with evidence expectations make submissions clearer and easier to score.

prompt: A team wants shorter assessments for low-risk suppliers. What should they configure?
choices: Disable questionnaires entirely
Use tier-based questionnaire templates with scoped sections by vendor risk level
Require full audits for all suppliers
Manually rewrite each questionnaire before sending
explanation: Tiered templates scale assessment depth according to inherent vendor risk and business criticality.

prompt: What is the best follow-up when a vendor provides incomplete questionnaire responses?
choices: Mark all unanswered controls as compliant
Close the assessment and wait for renewal
Initiate remediation or clarification tasks tied to unanswered or weak controls
Delete the questionnaire instance
explanation: Incomplete or weak responses require tracked follow-up to close evidence gaps before risk acceptance.

prompt: How should organizations handle recurring annual vendor questionnaires?
choices: Recreate every assessment manually
Automate reassessment schedules and reuse approved templates with updated control content
Skip reassessments if no incidents occurred
Assess only vendors that changed legal name
explanation: Scheduled reassessments with maintained templates improve consistency and reduce operational overhead.

prompt: What is the main goal of vulnerability prioritization in VR?
choices: Sort findings by import date only
Focus remediation on vulnerabilities with highest combined technical and business risk
Assign all vulnerabilities to one team
Close medium findings automatically
explanation: Prioritization combines severity, exploitability, and asset criticality to direct limited remediation capacity.

prompt: Which factor most improves prioritization accuracy for vulnerability items?
choices: Asset business criticality and exposure context
Analyst shift schedule
Number of dashboard tabs
Alphabetical ordering by vulnerability ID
explanation: Technical severity alone is insufficient; business context clarifies operational risk and urgency.

prompt: How do threat intelligence signals influence VR prioritization?
choices: They are ignored if CVSS is available
They increase urgency when vulnerabilities are known exploited or actively targeted
They replace remediation workflows
They remove need for asset ownership data
explanation: Known exploitation data can elevate remediation urgency beyond base severity values.

prompt: A security manager needs repeatable triage outcomes. What should be implemented?
choices: Ad hoc analyst decisions for each item
Risk-based assignment rules and defined SLAs by priority level
Single priority for all findings
Manual ticket routing through email
explanation: Rules and SLA targets create consistent prioritization and measurable execution discipline.

prompt: What is a common anti-pattern in vulnerability prioritization?
choices: Considering internet exposure in scoring
Incorporating exploit intelligence
Using business service context
Prioritizing solely by scanner severity without business impact weighting
explanation: Severity-only sorting often misses true operational risk and can misallocate remediation effort.

prompt: When a support center begins using CSM, what operational benefit is usually realized first?
choices: Case ownership, routing, and status visibility become standardized
All customer issues auto-resolve
HR tasks merge into customer cases by default
CMDB discovery stops creating records
explanation: Using CSM introduces structured case workflows and clearer accountability for service operations.

prompt: Which CSM feature supports consistent handling of repetitive customer issues?
choices: Catalog item depreciation
Template-based case creation and playbook-style workflows
Asset disposal rules
Manual free-form case states
explanation: Templates and guided workflows help agents execute recurring work with fewer errors and faster handoffs.

prompt: Why is omnichannel interaction capture valuable when using CSM?
choices: It separates customer context across channels
It preserves a unified timeline of customer engagement for better resolution continuity
It replaces entitlement rules
It prevents knowledge article use
explanation: A consolidated interaction history reduces duplicate questioning and improves handoffs across teams.

prompt: A manager wants to reduce escalations after adopting CSM. Which action is best?
choices: Define clear escalation criteria and automate routing to specialized queues
Route all cases to a single generic group
Disable priority fields on cases
Require phone-only support
explanation: Structured escalation rules ensure critical issues reach the right specialists quickly.

prompt: What indicator shows that teams are effectively using CSM knowledge deflection?
choices: Rising case reopen rate
Higher percentage of self-service resolutions before case creation
More manual routing overrides
Longer average first response time
explanation: Successful adoption includes increased self-service completion and reduced avoidable case volume.

prompt: In FSM scheduling, what is the primary value of defining technician skills?
choices: To suppress work order creation
To match work order tasks with qualified resources during assignment
To disable route optimization
To convert cases into incidents automatically
explanation: Skill profiles help ensure tasks are assigned to technicians who can successfully complete the work.

prompt: Why are service territories important in field scheduling?
choices: They determine payroll taxes only
They group agents by UI role
They define geographic assignment boundaries for efficient dispatching
They replace customer accounts
explanation: Territories constrain assignment regions and improve schedule efficiency and travel outcomes.

prompt: What scheduling input most directly supports realistic appointment commitments?
choices: Estimated task duration and customer availability windows
Technician favorite job type
Last dashboard refresh time
Count of open incidents
explanation: Duration estimates and availability windows are core constraints used to produce feasible schedules.

prompt: A dispatcher needs to reduce travel overhead. Which FSM capability should be emphasized?
choices: Manual first-come assignment only
Territory and route-aware scheduling optimization
Disabling appointment slots
Assigning all work to one technician
explanation: Optimization features account for geography and routing constraints to reduce unnecessary travel time.

prompt: What is a key reason to include buffer time in scheduling policies?
choices: To lower technician utilization to zero
To absorb real-world variation and avoid cascading delays across appointments
To prevent work notes from being entered
To remove SLA tracking
explanation: Reasonable buffers improve reliability when jobs overrun due to access, parts, or complexity issues.

prompt: In HRSD, why are HR case categories and topics configured?
choices: To sort vulnerabilities by CVSS score
To classify requests for routing, reporting, and fulfillment consistency
To replace employee profiles
To disable SLA tracking
explanation: Case categorization enables accurate assignment logic and better service analytics.

prompt: What does proper case assignment in HRSD help prevent?
choices: Cross-functional collaboration
Access control enforcement
Delayed handling caused by misrouted sensitive HR requests
Employee self-service adoption
explanation: Accurate routing reduces transfer delays and protects confidentiality by directing work to the right HR groups.

prompt: Which HR case feature supports consistent employee communications?
choices: Structured templates and activity timelines on case records
Free-form case records with no status model
Manual notes stored outside the platform
Disabled notifications
explanation: Templates and timeline visibility improve communication quality and auditability of HR interactions.

prompt: A compliance lead needs better oversight of employee requests. Which HRSD metric is most useful?
choices: Average time to resolve HR cases by service type
Number of vulnerable CIs
Technician travel distance
Count of discovery schedules
explanation: Resolution trend metrics by case type help identify process bottlenecks and policy compliance issues.

prompt: What is a common best practice for handling confidential HR case notes?
choices: Share all notes broadly for transparency
Use role-based access and case security controls to restrict sensitive detail visibility
Export all notes to public knowledge
Disable audit history for private fields
explanation: HR cases often include sensitive information and require strict visibility controls and traceability.
